summary: The test suite achieves strong coverage of core functionality—all three retention policies (max age, keep-latest-N per workflow, max total size) are well-tested with dry-run mode, error handling, and summary generation validated. Two comprehensive test files (test_cleanup.py unit tests and test_workflow.py integration tests) provide good organization with helpful fixtures and parametrized act scenarios. However, a critical design flaw exists: test_workflow_references_existing_script_files() asserts (PROJECT_ROOT / 'fixtures/artifacts.json').exists(), but this file is neither provided in the codebase nor created by any fixture—the test will fail before any actual testing occurs. Additionally, rigor is limited by absence of edge-case tests for negative/invalid parameters, timezone handling beyond UTC, artifact tie-breaking behavior, and comprehensive policy-combination scenarios (e.g., max_age + max_total_bytes together). The core unit tests are solid and would catch regressions in cleanup logic, but the failing assertion and missing edge-case coverage prevent confidence in the overall suite.
